article.body_preview:
Dhaka, Feb 15 (VOICE) Rampal power plant, a joint venture of Indian state-owned NTPC and Bangladesh Power Development Board (BPDB), will resume its operations from Wednesday.

Unit-1 of the plant, having 660 MW capacity, was forced to shut down on January 14 due to shortage of coal. The authorities of the power plant were unable to open any letter of credit (LC) to import coal due to the dollar crisis.

After a lot of persuasion at the policy level, LC-opening was allowed for importing coal and

llm_connections.evidence_quote: The authorities of the power plant were unable to open any letter of credit (LC) to import coal due to the dollar crisis.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly links a 'dollar crisis' to inability to open LCs for imports, directly matching the feature's focus on exchange-rate-related effects on import financing/costs.

verification.quote_best_match: The authorities of the power plant were unable to open any letter of credit (LC) to import coal due to the dollar crisis.
verification.judge_reasoning: The article contains the exact sentence stating the power plant authorities 'were unable to open any letter of credit (LC) to import coal due to the dollar crisis,' explicitly attributing the inability to open LCs (blocking import financing) to the dollar crisis and doing so at the company/plant level referenced by the feature.
